channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kano Police Impound 81 Vehicles for Missing Number Plates
item.description: The Kano State Police Command has impounded 81 vehicles for operating with covered, altered, or missing number plates as part of a statewide enforcement exercise aimed at improving public safety. Comm...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial Defense: Confidential Funds Approved by Marcos
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte opened with her defense team challenging the House of Representatives' allegations of misuse of confidential funds. Lead counsel Sheila Sison argue...

item.title: Ferrari's Vasseur Maintains Caution Amid F1 Championship Contention
item.description: The article discusses Ferrari's strategic approach to the Formula One championship, highlighting team principal Fred Vasseur's reluctance to hype their chances despite recent successes. Ferrari, who h...

item.title: South Africa Appoints Ayanda Dlodlo as Ambassador to France Ahead of Presidential Visit
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has appointed former state security minister Ayanda Dlodlo as South Africa’s new ambassador-designate to France, succeeding Nathi Mthethwa who passed away in September 2025. ...

item.title: Alexsandra Ann Diaz Sets Two Youth World Records at IWF Competition in Colombia
item.description: Alexsandra Ann Diaz, a young weightlifter from the Philippines, made history by setting two new youth world records during the 2026 IWF World Youth Weightlifting Championships held in Colombia. The co...

item.title: PAF officer killed while protecting woman, suspect arrested for identification parade
item.description: An anti-terrorism court in Islamabad has remanded suspect Saad Abbasi in judicial custody for 14 days to conduct an identification parade in the murder case of PAF Group Captain Asim Tariq. The incide...

item.title: Russian Aerial Strikes Target Kyiv Ahead of NATO Summit
item.description: Russian forces launched a major aerial attack on Kyiv overnight, killing at least 12 people and causing significant damage to residential areas. The strike occurred just a day before NATO leaders are ...

item.title: Tuk Tuk Operators Demand Apology for Derogatory Remarks on Transport Sector
item.description: Tuk tuk operators in Kenya have demanded a public apology from Professor Makau Mutua after he labeled their vehicles as 'pathetic contraptions.' The Tuk Tuk Operators Network (TTON) issued a seven-day...

item.title: EasyJet's $7.3-billion takeover by Castlelake faces regulatory hurdles
item.description: EasyJet shares rose 11.4% after agreeing to a £5.5-billion (US$7.34-billion) takeover bid from Castlelake, but investor concerns about regulatory approval persist. The deal, a 24% premium to EasyJet’s...

item.title: Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan strengthen bilateral ties through high-level diplomatic engagement
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari embarked on a historic four-day official visit to Kyrgyzstan, marking the first presidential visit to the country in 21 years. The trip, initiated by Kyrgyz President Sadyr ...

item.title: Tanzania's Political Repression and Fear of Police Violence
item.description: Tanzanians are increasingly fearful of potential police violence ahead of planned protests, with many citing traumatic memories of mass killings during last October's election as a reason for their ca...

item.title: Reflections on Life After a 9-Day Tech Detox
item.description: This article is a candid reflection on the author's experience of going without using their phone for nine days, highlighting both the challenges and unexpected benefits. The piece opens with a lighth...

item.title: Bristow deploys advanced helicopters for Nigerian offshore logistics and emergency services
item.description: Bristow Group has officially introduced the Airbus H160 helicopter into service in Nigeria, marking its first deployment in Africa's energy sector and rescue operations. The event, held on July 2, 202...

item.title: NIA Files Supplementary Chargesheet Against Hafiz Saeed in Pahalgam Terror Attack Case
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has filed a supplementary chargesheet against Pakistan-based terrorist Hafiz Saeed in the Pahalgam terror attack case, formally naming him as an accused for all...

item.title: Billionaire Patrice Motsepe's Investments in Africa's Economic Development
item.description: The article explores the strategic investments of South African billionaire Patrice Motsepe, who is leveraging his wealth to drive Africa's economic growth. Motsepe, a prominent figure in mining and b...

item.title: Ontario judge sentences man with intellectual disability to house arrest for sexually assaulting sister
item.description: An Ontario judge ruled that sentencing a man with intellectual disabilities to prison for sexually assaulting his 12-year-old sister would be 'cruel and unusual,' opting instead for a conditional sent...

item.title: Toyota Launches First Electric SUVs in South Africa
item.description: Toyota has expanded its electrified lineup in South Africa by introducing the bZ4X and bZ4X Touring, marking the brand's first fully battery-electric passenger vehicles in the country. These models, b...

item.title: Visually Impaired Cyclists Win Kaptagat Race, Highlighting Inclusion in Sports
item.description: Martin Kariuki, a visually impaired cyclist, and his pilot Samson Njenga won the para-cycling category at the 4th Kaptagat Cycling Challenge after a year of rigorous training. They improved from eight...

item.title: Cutix Plc Leads in Safety-Centric Cable Manufacturing Amid Fire Risks
item.description: Cutix Plc is making waves in Nigeria's industrial sector by prioritizing fire safety through high-quality cable manufacturing. The company addresses the growing problem of counterfeit cables that ofte...

item.title: Housing Market Slows as Sellers Face Challenges and Buyers Find Opportunities
item.description: The Australian housing market is experiencing a slowdown due to high inflation, rising interest rates, weakening economic growth, and declining consumer and business confidence. Cameron Kusher, chief ...
